--- Page 4 ---
I'm interested, what are the steps and resources to implement the CDED?2,7
Some testimonies from patients  
with Crohn's disease  
following the CDED...
6
•  Week 2 – Quick assessment: clinical symptoms, weight and treatment adherence.
•  Week 6 – Review: assessment of the response to phase 1. If the response is satisfactory, move to phase 2.
•  Week 12 – Review: assessment of clinical response and disease activity to decide whether to continue the CDED or transition to a varied and balanced diet.
Progression  
and follow-up  
of the CDED
•  No response to the CDED at week 6: an alternative therapy will be considered, or an addition to the 12-week protocol.
•  At the end of the 12-week period: according to medical advice and the patient's motivations, a return to a more diversified diet that respects nutritional recommendations and excludes ultra-processed foods, with a transition period and gradual reduction of the enteral nutrition solution.
Discontinuation  
of the CDED
•  Unintended weight loss: recalculation of energy intake and adjustment of the enteral nutrition solution
